name: Peanut Sauce (Nuoc Leo)
recipeIngredient: 1 Fresh red chile pepper, seeded and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
This peanut sauce is somewhat different from a Thai peanut sauce. It's not nearly as sweet and has no coconut milk in it. Most Vietnamese restaurants I've eaten at serve this along with Nuoc Cham and Carrot salad with Cha Gio type dishes.
1. Prepare the roasted peanuts and set aside.
2. Heat the oil in a small saucepan until hot.
3. Add the garlic, chili paste, and tomato paste to the hot oil. Cook for about 30 seconds or until the garlic is golden brown.
4. Add the broth, sugar, peanut butter, and hoisin sauce to the pan. Whisk to dissolve the peanut butter completely.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Reduce the heat and simmer for 3 minutes.
7. Divide the sauce among individual dipping bowls.
8. Garnish with ground peanuts and thinly sliced chile pepper.
9. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Yield: about 1 cup.
